{{Infobox ancient site | name = Matsumotozuka Kofun | native_name =жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі | native_name_lang = | image = Matsumotozuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsumotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsumotozuka | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Saito, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Matsumotozuka Kofun'''|жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the SannЕЌ neighborhood of the city of [[Saito, Miyazaki]] in [[Kyushu]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded [[paddy field]]s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The burial mound was built in two stages. The dimensions and design of the tumulus is very close to that of the Karusato Otsuka Kofun (late 5th century) one of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] located in [[Habikino, Osaka]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(е›ЅжЊ‡е®љеЏІи·Ўдє‹е…ё) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=е­¦з”џз¤ѕ |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Cylindrical and a smaller number of "morning glory-shaped" ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered from the moat. The date of construction is estimated to be around the end of the 5th century based on [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] artifacts. There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller burial mounds have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of [[Sadowara Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.saito.lg.jp/post_275.html Saito City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1851 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Matsumotozuka Kofun | native_name =жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі | native_name_lang = | image = Matsumotozuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsumotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsumotozuka | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Saito, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Matsumotozuka Kofun'''|жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the SannЕЌ neighborhood of the city of [[Saito, Miyazaki]] in [[Kyushu]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded [[paddy field]]s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The burial mound was built in two stages. The dimensions and design of the tumulus is very close to that of the Karusato Otsuka Kofun (late 5th century) one of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] located in [[Habikino, Osaka]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(е›ЅжЊ‡е®љеЏІи·Ўдє‹е…ё) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=е­¦з”џз¤ѕ |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Cylindrical and a smaller number of "morning glory-shaped" ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered from the moat. The date of construction is estimated to be around the end of the 5th century based on [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] artifacts. There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller burial mounds have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of [[Sadowara Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>

There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller burial mounds have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of [[Sadowara Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded [[paddy field]]s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The burial mound was built in two stages. The date of construction is estimated to be around the end of the 5th century based on [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] artifacts. There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller Kofuns have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of [[Sadowara Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Matsumotozuka Kofun'''|жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the SannЕЌ neighborhood of the city of [[Saito, Miyazaki]] in [[Kyushu]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded [[paddy field]]s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The Kofun was built in two stages. The dimensions and design of the tumulus is very close to that of the Karusato Otsuka Kofun (late 5th century) one of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] located in [[Habikino, Osaka]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(е›ЅжЊ‡е®љеЏІи·Ўдє‹е…ё) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=е­¦з”џз¤ѕ |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of [[Sadowara Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller Kofuns have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of [[Sadowara Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsumotozuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Matsumotozuka Kofun | native_name =жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі | native_name_lang = | image = Matsumotozuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsumotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsumotozuka | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Matsumotozuka Kofun'''|жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the SannЕЌ neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]] in [[Kyushu]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|4=ko|5=м „л°©н›„м›ђл¶„|6=zh|7=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊењ“еўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The Kofun was built in two stages. The dimensions and design of the tumulus is very close to that of the Karusato Otsuka Kofun (late 5th century) one of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] located in [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(е›ЅжЊ‡е®љеЏІи·Ўдє‹е…ё) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=е­¦з”џз¤ѕ |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Cylindrical and a smaller number of "morning glory-shaped" ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from the moat. The date of construction is estimated to be around the end of the 5th century based on [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] artifacts. There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller Kofuns have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of [[:en:Sadowara Station|Sadowara Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippo Main Line|Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]

{{nihongo|'''Matsumotozuka Kofun'''|жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the SannЕЌ neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]] in [[Kyushu]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|4=ko|5=м „л°©н›„м›ђл¶„|6=zh|7=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊењ“еўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The Kofun was built in two stages. The dimensions and design of the tumulus is very close to that of the Karusato Otsuka Kofun (late 5th century) one of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] located in [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(е›ЅжЊ‡е®љеЏІи·Ўдє‹е…ё) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=е­¦з”џз¤ѕ |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Cylindrical and a smaller number of "morning glory-shaped" ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from the moat. The date of construction is estimated to be around the end of the 5th century based on [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] artifacts. There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller Kofuns have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of [[:en:Sadowara Station|Sadowara Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippo Main Line|Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.saito.lg.jp/post_275.html Saito City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1851 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsumotozuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Matsumotozuka Kofun | native_name =жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі | native_name_lang = | image = Matsumotozuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsumotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsumotozuka | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Matsumotozuka Kofun'''|жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the SannЕЌ neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]] in [[Kyushu]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|4=ko|5=м „л°©н›„м›ђл¶„|6=zh|7=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊењ“еўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The Kofun was built in two stages. The dimensions and design of the tumulus is very close to that of the Karusato Otsuka Kofun (late 5th century) one of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] located in [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(е›ЅжЊ‡е®љеЏІи·Ўдє‹е…ё) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=е­¦з”џз¤ѕ |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Cylindrical and a smaller number of "morning glory-shaped" ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from the moat. The date of construction is estimated to be around the end of the 5th century based on [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] artifacts. There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller Kofuns have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of [[:en:Sadowara Station|Sadowara Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippo Main Line|Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.saito.lg.jp/post_275.html Saito City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1851 Miyazaki Cultural Heritage]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsumotozuka Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsumotozuka Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsumotozuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Matsumotozuka Kofun | native_name =жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі | native_name_lang = | image = Matsumotozuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsumotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsumotozuka | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Matsumotozuka Kofun'''|жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the SannЕЌ neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]] in [[Kyushu]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|4=ko|5=м „л°©н›„м›ђл¶„|6=zh|7=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊењ“еўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The Kofun was built in two stages. The dimensions and design of the tumulus is very close to that of the Karusato Otsuka Kofun (late 5th century) one of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] located in [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(е›ЅжЊ‡е®љеЏІи·Ўдє‹е…ё) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=е­¦з”џз¤ѕ |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Cylindrical and a smaller number of "morning glory-shaped" ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from the moat. The date of construction is estimated to be around the end of the 5th century based on [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] artifacts. There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller Kofuns have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of [[:en:Sadowara Station|Sadowara Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippo Main Line|Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]

{{nihongo|'''Matsumotozuka Kofun'''|жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the SannЕЌ neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]] in [[Kyushu]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|4=ko|5=м „л°©н›„м›ђл¶„|6=zh|7=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊењ“еўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The Kofun was built in two stages. The dimensions and design of the tumulus is very close to that of the Karusato Otsuka Kofun (late 5th century) one of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] located in [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(е›ЅжЊ‡е®љеЏІи·Ўдє‹е…ё) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=е­¦з”џз¤ѕ |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

Cylindrical and a smaller number of "morning glory-shaped" ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered from the moat. The date of construction is estimated to be around the end of the 5th century based on [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] artifacts. There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller Kofuns have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of [[:en:Sadowara Station|Sadowara Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippo Main Line|Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsumotozuka Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsumotozuka Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsumotozuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name = Matsumotozuka Kofun | native_name =жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі | native_name_lang = | image = Matsumotozuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsumotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsumotozuka | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Matsumotozuka Kofun'''|жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the SannЕЌ neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki}} in [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|4=ko|5=м „л°©н›„м›ђл¶„|6=zh|7=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊењ“еўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}}s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The Kofun was built in two stages. The dimensions and design of the tumulus is very close to that of the Karusato Otsuka Kofun (late 5th century) one of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] located in [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(е›ЅжЊ‡е®љеЏІи·Ўдє‹е…ё) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=е­¦з”џз¤ѕ |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Cylindrical and a smaller number of "morning glory-shaped" ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered from the moat. The date of construction is estimated to be around the end of the 5th century based on [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] artifacts. There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller Kofuns have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of {{ill|Sadowara Station|en|Sadowara Station|WD=Q5360779|lt=Sadowara Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} [[:en:Nippo Main Line|Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Matsumotozuka Kofun'''|жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the SannЕЌ neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki}} in [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|4=ko|5=м „л°©н›„м›ђл¶„|6=zh|7=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊењ“еўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}}s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The Kofun was built in two stages. The dimensions and design of the tumulus is very close to that of the Karusato Otsuka Kofun (late 5th century) one of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] located in [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(е›ЅжЊ‡е®љеЏІи·Ўдє‹е…ё) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=е­¦з”џз¤ѕ |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Cylindrical and a smaller number of "morning glory-shaped" ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered from the moat. The date of construction is estimated to be around the end of the 5th century based on [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] artifacts. There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller Kofuns have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of {{ill|Sadowara Station|en|Sadowara Station|WD=Q5360779|lt=Sadowara Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} [[:en:Nippo Main Line|Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|WD=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}

{{nihongo|'''Matsumotozuka Kofun'''|жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=ZenpЕЌkЕЌenfun}}, located in the SannЕЌ neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}} in [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|4=ko|5=м „л°©н›„м›ђл¶„|6=zh|7=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊењ“еўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field|1=Paddy field}}s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The Kofun was built in two stages. The dimensions and design of the tumulus is very close to that of the Karusato Otsuka Kofun (late 5th century) one of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] located in [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(е›ЅжЊ‡е®љеЏІи·Ўдє‹е…ё) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=е­¦з”џз¤ѕ |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Cylindrical and a smaller number of "morning glory-shaped" ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered from the moat. The date of construction is estimated to be around the end of the 5th century based on [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] artifacts. There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller Kofuns have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of {{ill|Sadowara Station|en|Sadowara Station|WD=Q5360779|lt=Sadowara Station|1=Sadowara Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} [[:en:Nippo Main Line|Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsumotozuka Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsumotozuka Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsumotozuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=ZenpЕЌkЕЌenfun}} | name = Matsumotozuka Kofun | native_name =жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі | native_name_lang = | image = Matsumotozuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Matsumotozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Matsumotozuka | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|06|08.7|N|131|22|29|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Matsumotozuka Kofun'''|жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=ZenpЕЌkЕЌenfun}}, located in the SannЕЌ neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}} in [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|4=ko|5=м „л°©н›„м›ђл¶„|6=zh|7=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊењ“еўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field|1=Paddy field}}s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The Kofun was built in two stages. The dimensions and design of the tumulus is very close to that of the Karusato Otsuka Kofun (late 5th century) one of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] located in [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(е›ЅжЊ‡е®љеЏІи·Ўдє‹е…ё) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=е­¦з”џз¤ѕ |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Cylindrical and a smaller number of "morning glory-shaped" ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered from the moat. The date of construction is estimated to be around the end of the 5th century based on [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] artifacts. There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller Kofuns have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of {{ill|Sadowara Station|en|Sadowara Station|WD=Q5360779|lt=Sadowara Station|1=Sadowara Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} [[:en:Nippo Main Line|Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|WD=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}

It is located surrounded {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|qid=Q842623|lt=Paddy field|1=Paddy field}}s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. {{nihongo|'''Matsumotozuka Kofun'''|жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|ZenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun|en|ZenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=ZenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun}}, located in the SannЕЌ neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|qid=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}} in [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139977|title=жќѕжњ¬еЎљеЏ¤еўі|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Funazuka Kofun'''|и€№еЎљеЏ¤еўі|}} as it appears to be shaped like a ship.
==Overview== The Matsumotozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun''|е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|1=ZenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun|2=ja|3=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|4=ko|5=м „л°©н›„м›ђл¶„|6=zh|7=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊењ“еўі|lt=ZenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=ZenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun|1=ZenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=ZenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun|1=[[ZenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun]]|2=е‰Ќж–№еѕЊе††еўі|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located surrounded {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|qid=Q842623|lt=Paddy field|1=Paddy field}}s, on the south side of a plateau called Matsumotobara, which stretches 1.4 kilometers on the norths side of the Sanno River, and west of the city center to the southeast. The tumulus has a total length of about 104 meters, with the diameter of the posterior circular portion at 61 meters, with a height of 7.6 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion being 79 meters, with a height of eight meters. There is also a trapezoidal structure on the west side, presumably a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat and embankment, which was discovered during [[archaeological excavation]]s, conducted in 1986 and 1987. Together with the moat, the total length is 149 meters. The Kofun was built in two stages. The dimensions and design of the tumulus is very close to that of the Karusato Otsuka Kofun (late 5th century) one of the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] located in [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(е›ЅжЊ‡е®љеЏІи·Ўдє‹е…ё) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=е­¦з”џз¤ѕ |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Cylindrical and a smaller number of "morning glory-shaped" ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered from the moat. The date of construction is estimated to be around the end of the 5th century based on [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] artifacts. There is no other ''zenpЕЌ-kЕЌen-fun'' from the same period, including the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], in the Hitotsuse River basin, and the tumulus is also highly unusual in that it was built on alluvial land rather than on a plateau. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus was part of a small cluster of {{nihongo|''enpun''|е††еўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round mounds of the same period known as the Sanno Kofun Cluster, but almost all of these smaller Kofuns have now been destroyed.
The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of {{ill|Sadowara Station|en|Sadowara Station|qid=Q5360779|lt=Sadowara Station|1=Sadowara Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} [[:en:Nippo Main Line|Nippo Main Line]], or five kilometers southeast of the Saito Kofun Cluster<ref name="Shiseki"/>
